violations.text: The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

The concentration of the sanitizer at the 3 bay sink was not at recommended levels. Service was called during inspection. Dishwasher will be used to clean and sanitize equipment. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Chicken broth, which was previously cooked and cooled, was only reheated to 142 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. Chicken broth was reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Rice and cooked Brussels sprouts observed held at 50-52 degrees Fahrenheit overnight in the prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. Discussed not overfilling prep unit containers with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Cooked shrimp was placed hot in the prep unit in covered plastic container greater than four inches deep. Creme brulee cooled previously observed in container greater than 4 inches. Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of ( ), ( ) was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

The dessert refrigerator in the bar area was not in good repair, not maintaining an ambient temperature of 41 or below. Foods requiring refrigeration are not to be stored in dessert refrigerator until it is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the bar. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: can opener base mixer charcuterie prep unit cold drawer unit beneath grill ice machine . New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
The oil recycling bin has an accumulation of grease and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2026
The dumpster enclosure has an accumulation of trash and debris.The dumpster enclosure must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2026

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored on the clean towel storage rack. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026
Bolognese and marinara observed with date marks exceeding 7 days. Disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025
Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler and freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025
Water observed in numerous clean food containers on clean storage racks. After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils must be air dried. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the basement storage area. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2025

Single service items observed on shelves less than 6 inches above the floor in the basement storage area. Single service items must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the can opener base, and ice machine have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. Broken glass shards observed in bar refrigerator, and speed rail at unused ice bine at bar. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025
A large build-up of ice observed on the fan soffit in the walk in freezer. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

The dishwasher failed to wash his/her hands between loading soiled and unloading clean dishes from the dishwasher. Food employees must clean their hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

The hand washing sink in the dough prep room is lacking proper signage. A sign or poster reminding food employees to wash their hands must be placed at all hand sinks used by food employees. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024
The hand-washing sink in the basement dough kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

Calamari bread mix bowl had an accumulation of food and dried spills from last time used. The surfaces of equipment that contacts time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as meats and cheeses must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized at least every four hours. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

Vegetable stock was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

Working containers of numerous dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

Scoops observed in dry ingredients with the handle in the food. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024
Clean towels observed stored on the floor in the employee locker area. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024
Water observed in numerous stacked plastic food containers. After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils must be air dried. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the storage area. Pizza boxes observed stored on the floor. Single service items must be stored in a clean dry location, where they are not subject to dust, splash, or other contamination, and at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024

Several wood butcher blocks used for serving steak were not in good repair, with cracks and the wood separating, impacting their ability to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 01/03/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the mixer, ice machine, knife rack, portable fan, dough mixer, dough refrigerator, NS scales in dough room, have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024
Hot water was not available at the hand wash sink in the dough prep room. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024
The fan cover in the walk in freezer has an accumulation of ice. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2024
The fan covers in the walk in cooler have an accumulation of dust and mold. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2024
